changes_made: Changed k8s_service_type_loadbalancer_restricted to k8s_service_type_loadbalancer_not_exposed to better reflect the security control
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the PCI DSS control 2.2.3, which requires different security levels for different functions. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but the function related to service exposure needed refinement for clarity. The changes ensure the function names are descriptive and align with the intended security checks.
title: (continued) If a system component contains primary functions that need different security levels,...
step1_reasoning: The PCI DSS control 2.2.3 requires different security levels for different functions within a system component. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that roles and permissions are appropriately restricted (RBAC), network access is controlled (NetworkPolicies), pods are not running with unnecessary privileges (Pod Security), and services are not exposed unnecessarily (Service Type). These checks ensure that different components or functions within a Kubernetes cluster have appropriate security boundaries.
